title: sida rivulicola
intro: Sida rivulicola, also known as the river sida or rivuleted sida, is a plant species in the mallow family, Malvaceae. It is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Sida rivulicola are often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, which are used to treat various ailments.
